access.anon: Can browse calendar information on public section detail pages.
access.user: Can browse the personal calendar view.
access.admin: Has no special calendar view separate from regular users.
access.agent: Can read timeline information with the same semantics through OAuth REST and MCP.

rules.views: Provides selectable semester, month, and week views.
rules.retired-sections: Current/public collection calendars, personal calendar views, feeds, and calendar event tools exclude retired Sections. A direct single-Section detail/calendar URL remains available as a historical reference, and retirement does not delete underlying schedules, exams, homeworks, or subscription relationships.
rules.mobile-agenda-first: At mobile widths, the personal calendar presents the selected week as a vertically readable agenda with time, title, type, and available location context. Previous week, today, next week, and iCal actions remain directly accessible without page-level horizontal scrolling; desktop retains the selectable grid views.
rules.week-starts-sunday: The week view starts on Sunday.
rules.event-card-types: Calendar event cards uniformly carry course, exam, homework, and todo events.

tools.name: workspace_calendar_event_list
notes: Returns course, exam, homework, and todo events unified by date range, directly corresponding to the personal calendar view.
Each item in events[] has type, at, and payload; payload corresponds to ScheduleEntry, HomeworkItem, ExamEntry, or TodoItem based on type.
The deprecated summary input returns the same compact events[] structure as default; full adds raw event fields without changing the collection type.

tools.name: workspace_calendar_timeline_get
tools.returns: { range: { from: DateTime, to: DateTime }, total: Int, events: CalendarEvent[] }

tools.name: workspace_schedule_list
notes: Omitting semesterId reads all subscribed semesters; pass a semesterId to recover one current or past term.

tools.name: workspace_exam_list
notes: Omitting semesterId reads all subscribed semesters; pass a semesterId to recover one current or past term.
